bugs.root_cause: data: summaryData, isLoading: summaryLoading â†’ boardData, isLoading: summaryLoading; queryKey: ['daily-summary'], â†’ ['housekeeping-board-frontdesk', today],; queryFn: () => reportsApi.getDailySummary(), â†’ () => housekeepingApi.getBoard(today, undefined, false),
bugs.fix: Changed data: summaryData, isLoading: summaryLoading â†’ boardData, isLoading: summaryLoading; queryKey: ['daily-summary'], â†’ ['housekeeping-board-frontdesk', today],; queryFn: () => reportsApi.getDailySummary(), â†’ () => housekeepingApi.getBoard(today, undefined, false),

bugs.error_message: Type error
bugs.root_cause: Missing or incorrect type annotation
bugs.fix: Added type assertion/annotation

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: role should be canManageStaff
bugs.root_cause: Used "role" instead of "canManageStaff"
bugs.fix: Changed role â†’ canManageStaff

bugs.error_message: Significant refactor of
bugs.root_cause: 4 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 10â†’16 lines (4 removed)

bugs.error_message: IDOR: GET /v1/hotels/{hotel_id}/departments returns 200 for any authenticated user regardless of their hotel_id
bugs.root_cause: list_hotel_departments did not compare current_user.hotel_id to the hotel_id URL param. The supabase client uses service role key (bypasses RLS), so the only guard is the app-level tenant filter. The endpoint filtered by eq('tenant_id', hotel_id) but never verified the caller owns that hotel_id.
bugs.fix: Added 'if current_user.hotel_id != hotel_id: raise HTTPException(403)' at the top of list_hotel_departments, matching the pattern used by get_hotel and update_hotel.

bugs.error_message: Cross-tenant comment injection: POST /tasks/{task_id}/comments inserts without verifying task_id belongs to caller's hotel
bugs.root_cause: add_task_comment inserted into task_comments with the caller's tenant_id but the supplied task_id without first checking the task belongs to their hotel. An attacker with a known cross-tenant task UUID could inject comments visible when that hotel's users view the task (since the get_task query embeds task_comments by task_id without tenant filter on the join).
bugs.fix: Added a pre-flight .eq('id', task_id).eq('tenant_id', current_user.hotel_id) query before the insert. Returns 404 if task not found in caller's tenant.

bugs.error_message: HTTP 500 instead of 404 on GET /{resource}/{nonexistent_uuid} for rooms, tasks, work-orders, lost-found, assets
bugs.root_cause: When fetching a single resource by UUID that does not exist in the tenant's namespace, the supabase-py maybe_single() + select with joined relations raises an unhandled exception that is caught by the generic_exception_handler (not APIError handler), yielding 500 INTERNAL_ERROR. No data is leaked but callers receive an incorrect status code.
bugs.fix: Not fixed yet â€” needs Railway log inspection to identify the exact exception type, then either catch it explicitly or wrap the maybe_single().execute() call in a try/except to surface 404.

bugs.error_message: Significant refactor of
bugs.root_cause: 8 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 10â†’4 lines (8 removed)

bugs.error_message: POST /v1/internal/billing/monthly-trueup returns HTTP 422 PGRST200
bugs.root_cause: credit_ledger and subscriptions have no FK relationship; .select("*, subscriptions(...)") attempted a PostgREST join that does not exist in the schema cache, raising PGRST200.
bugs.fix: Removed the embedded join. Now fetches credit_ledger rows first, then queries subscriptions separately per tenant_id using maybe_single().

bugs.error_message: POST /v1/internal/reports/daily-summary-email: AttributeError NoneType has no attribute data (all 9 hotels)
bugs.root_cause: Two issues: (1) supabase-py 2.5.0 maybe_single().execute() returns None (not APIResponse) when no user_profiles row found for GM; accessing .data on None crashes. (2) logbook_entries insert omitted required NOT NULL department_id column, would always fail at DB level.
bugs.fix: (1) Added None guard: gm_profile_data = (gm_profile.data if gm_profile else None) or {}. (2) Replaced logbook insert stub with logger.info() since Resend/SendGrid not yet integrated and department_id is NOT NULL.

bugs.error_message: POST /v1/internal/logbook/shift-summary: anthropic.AuthenticationError 401 invalid x-api-key
bugs.root_cause: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in Railway production environment is invalid or revoked. The key sk-ant-api03-hmcj3m... returns 401 from Anthropic API.
bugs.fix: PENDING: Rotate the Anthropic API key and update the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY variable in Railway production API service.

bugs.error_message: Incorrect value in code
bugs.root_cause: Had "id, tenant_id, end_time"
bugs.fix: Changed to "id, tenant_id, department_id, end_time"

bugs.error_message: PGRST204 generated_by_ai column not found in shift_summaries
bugs.root_cause: Upsert included phantom columns (generated_by_ai, model_used, tasks_completed, open_work_orders, logbook_entries_count) not in schema. Also missing required department_id.
bugs.fix: Removed phantom columns, packed metrics into stats JSONB, extracted department_id from shift_result.data, added maybe_single None guard.

bugs.error_message: 42P10 no unique constraint matching ON CONFLICT (tenant_id, shift_id)
bugs.root_cause: shift_summaries has no UNIQUE(tenant_id,shift_id) constraint. Duplicate check already done in internal.py before calling generate_shift_summary.
bugs.fix: Changed upsert to plain insert -- internal.py existence check prevents duplicates without needing a DB constraint.

bugs.error_message: Supabase Realtime postgres_changes subscriptions on room_status and room_assignments have no tenant_id filter — all tenants' row payloads (including guest names, VIP flags, checkin/checkout times) are broadcast to every hotel's browser sessions
bugs.root_cause: Both Realtime subscriptions use .on('postgres_changes', { event: '*', schema: 'public', table: 'room_status' }) without a filter clause. Supabase postgres_changes does NOT enforce RLS on the WebSocket payload — missing filter means every subscriber receives the full new/old row data for ALL tenants. RLS on room_status (migration 016) only applies to direct DB queries, not Realtime messages.
bugs.fix: Add filter: `tenant_id=eq.${hotelId}` to both .on() calls in RoomStatusBoard.tsx and housekeeping/page.tsx. Requires hotelId from useAuthStore or useHotelStore.

bugs.error_message: Engineering Work Orders page has no Supabase Realtime subscription — spec requires it; work_orders table is also not in supabase_realtime publication
bugs.root_cause: Migration 025 only adds room_status and room_assignments to supabase_realtime. The work-orders page uses refetchInterval: 30_000 (polling) with no WebSocket channel. Engineers working concurrently miss each other's updates for up to 30 seconds.
bugs.fix: Add migration to: ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE work_orders; Then add useEffect subscription in work-orders/page.tsx with filter: tenant_id=eq.${hotelId}.

bugs.error_message: Missing error handling in getHotelIdFromToken
bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
bugs.fix: Added try/catch block

bugs.error_message: Missing guard clause
bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: !hotelId
bugs.fix: Added guard clause: if (!hotelId)

bugs.error_message: Missing error handling in getHotelIdFromToken
bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
bugs.fix: Added try/catch block | Also: function getHotelIdFromToken(token: string | undefined): str; try { return JSON.parse(atob(token!.split('.')[1]))?.hotel_i

bugs.error_message: Missing guard clause
bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: !hotelId
bugs.fix: Added guard clause: if (!hotelId)

bugs.error_message: Incorrect value in code
bugs.root_cause: Had 'inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 px-
bugs.fix: Changed to 'inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 px-

bugs.error_message: Incorrect value in code
bugs.root_cause: Had 'group flex items-center gap-2.5 px-3 py-2 text-sm
bugs.fix: Changed to 'group flex items-center gap-2.5 px-3 py-2.5 text-

bugs.error_message: Reports 'Maintenance' tab clipped on phone — unreachable by GM on mobile
bugs.root_cause: Tab nav wrapper <div> had no overflow-x-auto; 3+ tabs overflow off-screen on viewports <500px
bugs.fix: Added overflow-x-auto to wrapper div and min-w-max + shrink-0 to nav/buttons

bugs.error_message: Settings 'Front Desk', 'Roles', 'Inspections' tabs clipped on all viewports
bugs.root_cause: Tab wrapper had no overflow-x-auto; tabs without shrink-0 wrap or overflow off screen
bugs.fix: Added overflow-x-auto to wrapper div and shrink-0 to each tab button

bugs.error_message: Settings State input shows 'T.' (truncated 'TX') on phones — grid-cols-6 makes State column ~50px wide
bugs.root_cause: City/State/ZIP used grid-cols-6 with col-span-1 for State; on 375px phone the State column is ~50px leaving ~26px text area — too narrow
bugs.fix: Changed to grid-cols-3 sm:grid-cols-6 so City/State/ZIP stack properly on mobile; State + ZIP each get adequate width

bugs.error_message: Housekeeping status filter chips: 'Inspected' and 'Clean' partially hidden with no scroll affordance
bugs.root_cause: overflow-x-auto was present but no visual cue signaled that the chip row was scrollable
bugs.fix: Wrapped chips in relative container; added right-edge fade gradient (pointer-events-none) as scroll hint

bugs.error_message: Tasks 'In Progress' tab wraps to 2 lines on 375px phones
bugs.root_cause: Tab container used w-fit without overflow-x-auto; text wrapped on narrow screens
bugs.fix: Added overflow-x-auto + max-w-full to container; added shrink-0 + whitespace-nowrap to tab buttons

bugs.error_message: AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'data' in check_and_deduct_credits
bugs.root_cause: supabase-py 2.5.0 maybe_single().execute() returns None (not APIResponse) when no row exists. Code accessed ledger_result.data and sub_result.data without guarding against None result object.
bugs.fix: Guarded all maybe_single() results: 'if not (ledger_result and ledger_result.data)', '(sub_result.data or {}).get(...)  if sub_result else default', 'ledger = (ledger_result.data if ledger_result else None) or {}'

bugs.error_message: PATCH /v1/work-orders/{wo_id} returns 200 with data:null for nonexistent work order
bugs.root_cause: PATCH /{wo_id} had no pre-flight existence check. Supabase UPDATE against a non-matching row returns empty data silently; code returned {'data': None} with 200.
bugs.fix: Added maybe_single() existence check before UPDATE, raises 404 if not found. Consistent with other WO mutation endpoints.

bugs.error_message: Unauthenticated requests return 403 instead of 401
bugs.root_cause: HTTPBearer() with default auto_error=True raises HTTP 403 when Authorization header is missing. RFC 7235 requires 401 with WWW-Authenticate header for missing credentials.
bugs.fix: Set HTTPBearer(auto_error=False) and added explicit 401 + WWW-Authenticate header in get_current_user and get_current_user_no_hotel.

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: data should be and
bugs.root_cause: Used "data" instead of "and"
bugs.fix: Changed data → and

bugs.error_message: column room_readiness_predictions.id does not exist (PostgREST 42703)
bugs.root_cause: room_readiness_predictions uses room_id as its PRIMARY KEY (not id). _get_7day_stats was selecting 'id' for the count query causing PostgREST 42703 on every /ai/insights and insight_query copilot call.
bugs.fix: Changed select('id', count='exact') to select('room_id', count='exact') on the room_readiness_predictions query.

bugs.error_message: model: claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022 not found (Anthropic 404)
bugs.root_cause: claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022 is not available on the current Anthropic API key. Model superseded by Claude 4.x family.
bugs.fix: Replaced all claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022 references with claude-sonnet-4-6 across 4 files.

bugs.error_message: AI service error: Error code: 429 - insufficient_quota (OpenAI)
bugs.root_cause: OPENAI_API_KEY was blank in Railway. After adding the key, the provided key had no billing/quota on the OpenAI account. Raw OpenAI error was leaking to clients via the generic except block.
bugs.fix: Added specific except clause for openai.RateLimitError and openai.AuthenticationError (and Anthropic equivalents) that returns clean 503 instead of exposing raw provider error messages.

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: AuthenticationStatusError should be AuthenticationError
bugs.root_cause: Used "AuthenticationStatusError" instead of "AuthenticationError"
bugs.fix: Changed AuthenticationStatusError → AuthenticationError

bugs.error_message: Significant refactor of
bugs.root_cause: 5 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 28→60 lines (5 removed)

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: 30_000 should be 60_000
bugs.root_cause: Used "30_000" instead of "60_000"

bugs.error_message: CSS fix: refetchInterval
bugs.root_cause: refetchInterval: 30_000, → 60_000,
bugs.fix: Changed refetchInterval: 30_000, → 60_000,

bugs.error_message: CSS fix: refetchInterval
bugs.root_cause: refetchInterval: 30_000, → 60_000,
bugs.fix: Changed refetchInterval: 30_000, → 60_000,

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: 30_000 should be 120_000
bugs.root_cause: Used "30_000" instead of "120_000"

bugs.error_message: Significant refactor of
bugs.root_cause: 2 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 18→56 lines (2 removed)

bugs.error_message: API smoke suite failed 3 tests after auth/work-order hardening
bugs.root_cause: Tests still expect missing auth to return 403 and cross-tenant PATCH work-order to be a 200 data:null no-op. Current production behavior is stricter: missing auth returns 401 and cross-tenant/nonexistent work-order PATCH returns 404.
bugs.fix: Updated smoke tests to expect 401 for unauthenticated/invalid auth and 404 for cross-tenant work-order PATCH.

bugs.error_message: PATCH /v1/lost-found/{id} returns 422 PGRST204 when claimed_by_contact is sent
bugs.root_cause: Endpoint allowlist includes claimed_by_contact, but the production lost_found_items table does not expose that column in PostgREST schema. Status=claimed and claimed_by_name work; adding claimed_by_contact fails.
bugs.fix: Added migration 033 to create lost_found_items.claimed_by_contact and notify PostgREST to reload schema.

bugs.error_message: Supabase Realtime subscribed to room_status but did not receive tenant-filtered UPDATE payload during production room status mutation
bugs.root_cause: Production validation confirmed channel status SUBSCRIBED, API PATCH /rooms/{id}/status returned 200, and room was restored, but no postgres_changes event arrived within 12 seconds. Publication/filter/auth configuration needs investigation.
bugs.fix: Added migration 033 to idempotently add room_status and room_assignments to supabase_realtime and set FULL replica identity for reliable tenant-filtered UPDATE payloads.

bugs.error_message: Dashboard emits Next.js RSC prefetch console errors for several routes, then falls back to browser navigation
bugs.root_cause: Console pass captured Failed to fetch RSC payload messages for /tasks, /housekeeping, /guest-requests, /lost-found, and /engineering while on /dashboard. Navigation succeeds, but production console is noisy.
bugs.fix: Disabled Next Link prefetch on Sidebar main, subnav, and bottom navigation links to prevent noisy dashboard RSC prefetch fetches.

bugs.error_message: Legacy -869a Railway web host returns 404 Application not found
bugs.root_cause: Project memory/infrastructure notes still reference the older -869a Railway URL, but the canonical production web URL is https://patelrepweb-production.up.railway.app.
bugs.fix: Updated docs, API CORS allowlist, web production env, and e2e helpers to use https://patelrepweb-production.up.railway.app and https://api-production-130b.up.railway.app.

bugs.error_message: Supabase Realtime did not emit tenant-filtered room_status UPDATE after PATCH /v1/rooms/{room_id}/status
bugs.root_cause: Production needed an explicit idempotent Realtime publication and replica identity hardening pass for room_status/room_assignments so filtered UPDATE streams have the row images Realtime needs.
bugs.fix: Applied migration 033 to Supabase: set REPLICA IDENTITY FULL and ensured room_status and room_assignments are in supabase_realtime.

bugs.error_message: PATCH /v1/lost-found/{id} returned 422 PGRST204 when claimed_by_contact was sent
bugs.root_cause: The API/frontend contract allowed claimed_by_contact but production lost_found_items did not have the column in the schema cache.
bugs.fix: Applied migration 033 to Supabase: added claimed_by_contact TEXT, documented it in the schema spec, and sent NOTIFY pgrst to refresh PostgREST.

bugs.error_message: Dashboard emitted failed Next.js RSC prefetch console errors
bugs.root_cause: Sidebar links were eligible for automatic App Router prefetching, which produced noisy failed RSC payload fetches on the production dashboard even though manual navigation succeeded.
bugs.fix: Set prefetch={false} on Sidebar main nav, subnav, and bottom nav links.

bugs.error_message: API smoke tests had stale auth and work-order status expectations
bugs.root_cause: Tests still allowed old HTTPBearer 403 behavior and old cross-tenant work-order PATCH no-op behavior after API hardening.
bugs.fix: Updated smoke tests to expect 401 auth failures and 404 for cross-tenant work-order PATCH.

bugs.error_message: Stale Railway production URLs remained in docs/config/helpers
bugs.root_cause: Infrastructure notes and fallback URLs referenced retired Railway hosts after production moved to the canonical web/API domains.
bugs.fix: Replaced stale Railway references with https://patelrepweb-production.up.railway.app and https://api-production-130b.up.railway.app; grep confirms retired host strings are gone.

bugs.error_message: Live cross-tenant probes returned HTTP 500 instead of 404/403 for no-match tenant-scoped resources
bugs.root_cause: Several router handlers call maybe_single().execute() and then access .data without guarding for supabase-py returning None when the tenant-scoped query has no rows.
bugs.fix: FIXED 2026-05-12: added `if not result or not result.data` guards across rooms/logbook/work_orders/guest_requests/sop no-match maybe_single paths; verified in production with 404 probes.

bugs.error_message: PATCH /v1/lost-found/{id} returns 200 with data:null for cross-tenant IDs
bugs.root_cause: update_lost_found_item performs a tenant-scoped update but does not check whether any row matched before returning 200.
bugs.fix: FIXED 2026-05-12: update_lost_found_item now checks update result rows and raises 404 when no current-tenant item matched; verified in production.

bugs.error_message: PATCH/DELETE /v1/staff/{user_id} returns 200 for cross-tenant user IDs without mutating
bugs.root_cause: update_staff and deactivate_staff run tenant-scoped updates but do not verify a matching user_roles row was updated, so cross-tenant IDs produce success responses.
bugs.fix: FIXED 2026-05-12: update_staff and deactivate_staff now check update result rows and raise 404 when no current-tenant staff row matched; verified in production.

bugs.error_message: POST /v1/webhooks/opera with unknown/minimal hotel payload returns HTTP 500
bugs.root_cause: opera_webhook calls maybe_single().execute() for opera_credentials and then accesses creds.data without guarding for supabase-py returning None when no connected Opera credentials match.
bugs.fix: FIXED 2026-05-12: opera_webhook now guards `if not creds or not creds.data` and returns the documented ignored response for unknown/unconnected hotel IDs; verified in production.

bugs.error_message: POST /v1/sop/query returns HTTP 500 when OpenAI quota/rate limit is hit
bugs.root_cause: SOP query path lets OpenAI RateLimitError/insufficient_quota escape to the generic exception handler. The main copilot task path correctly maps provider failures to a friendly 503, but SOP RAG does not.
bugs.fix: FIXED 2026-05-12: SOP query catches OpenAI/Anthropic quota/auth failures and returns generic HTTP 503; provider-unavailable path now logs a warning without traceback; verified in production.

bugs.error_message: Production Playwright suite failed 5 tests despite focused route audit passing
bugs.root_cause: Lost & Found tests use exact accessible name "Claimed" even the button includes a count ("Claimed 1"). RBAC helper did not load NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY outside the app env, causing Supabase auth 401. Mobile login tests inherited authenticated storage state and landed on Dashboard instead of Login.
bugs.fix: FIXED 2026-05-12: Playwright Lost & Found tab locators tolerate count badges, RBAC helper loads env files and normalizes /v1, and mobile login tests run with clean storage; targeted suites pass.

bugs.error_message: Railway deploy can target the wrong monorepo service/Dockerfile when workspace path and root railway.toml are mixed
bugs.root_cause: Using Railway MCP deploy with apps/api workspace path caused Railway to read the root service manifest and start a web Dockerfile build for the API service. The bad deployment was removed after a corrected CLI upload.
bugs.fix: Use `railway up apps/api --path-as-root --service api --detach -m "..."` and verify deployment metadata shows builder DOCKERFILE, dockerfilePath Dockerfile, and healthcheckPath /health before treating it as valid.

bugs.error_message: rg --files apps/api/tests e2e failed with 'Access is denied' in PowerShell
bugs.root_cause: The ripgrep executable was present but not executable from this workspace/session, so validation file discovery had to fall back to Get-ChildItem.
bugs.fix: Not fixed in this session; used PowerShell Get-ChildItem fallback for discovery.

bugs.error_message: PATCH /v1/rooms/{room_id}/status returns HTTP 500 on IN_PROGRESS -> CLEAN during production validation
bugs.root_cause: _update_housekeeper_profile runs only on IN_PROGRESS -> CLEAN and still has a maybe_single().execute() result path that accesses .data when supabase-py returns None, producing AttributeError NoneType has no attribute data after the room_status update succeeds.
bugs.fix: FIXED 2026-05-13: guarded maybe_single result access in _update_housekeeper_profile and made the non-critical housekeeper profile update log-and-continue after the status update/history insert. Verified production PATCH IN_PROGRESS -> CLEAN returns 200.

bugs.error_message: Production dashboard emits Next.js Failed to fetch RSC payload console errors for prefetched dashboard routes
bugs.root_cause: Authenticated dashboard navigation/prefetch still attempts RSC payload fetches for routes including /tasks, /guest-requests, /engineering, /lost-found, /housekeeping, /staff, /ai, /scheduling, and /logbook; fetch fails and falls back to browser navigation.
bugs.fix: FIXED 2026-05-13: disabled prefetch on dashboard quick links and role dashboards behind middleware. Production browser probe showed 0 RSC console errors and 0 bad RSC responses.

bugs.error_message: Two-session production realtime check did not show a visible room_status update in the second browser session within 15 seconds
bugs.root_cause: A visible room 134 was changed DIRTY -> PICKUP through the API with HTTP 200 and restored afterward, but a second logged-in /housekeeping browser session did not render the new status within the observation window. Either the Realtime subscription is not firing in production or the UI is not applying the payload to the current board state.
bugs.fix: FIXED 2026-05-13: stabilized Supabase Realtime client creation/auth, applied room_status payloads directly to Zustand/React Query caches, and added a 10s React Query refetch fallback for supervisor and housekeeper boards. Production browser freshness probe passed for cross-session room status updates.

bugs.error_message: Browser-use Node REPL could not initialize: Node runtime v22.18.0 is below required >= v22.22.0
bugs.root_cause: The browser-use skill requires a newer Node runtime for node_repl than the node.exe resolved in this environment.
bugs.fix: Not fixed in this session; fell back to shell Playwright audits for browser validation.

bugs.error_message: Production /logbook emitted React hydration errors on desktop and mobile route audit
bugs.root_cause: Logbook computed todayIso() during server render and again in the browser. Around UTC/Texas date boundaries, Railway and the hotel user's browser can render different selected dates, causing React hydration mismatch errors 418/423/425.
bugs.fix: FIXED 2026-05-13: moved logbook today/selectedDate initialization to a client-only mount effect, disabled entry query until selectedDate is available, and rendered a deterministic skeleton before mount. Redeployed web and verified 44/44 desktop/mobile route audit plus full Playwright suite passed.

bugs.error_message: apps/mobile npm install failed with ERESOLVE because react-test-renderer resolved to 18.3.1 while React is pinned to 18.2.0
bugs.root_cause: react-test-renderer was declared as ^18.2.0, allowing npm to install 18.3.1. Expo SDK 51 uses React 18.2.0, and react-test-renderer 18.3.1 has a peer dependency on React ^18.3.1.
bugs.fix: Pinned react-test-renderer to exact version 18.2.0 and generated apps/mobile/package-lock.json from a successful npm install.

bugs.error_message: apps/mobile TypeScript failed TS2352 on direct casts from real module types to jest.Mock
bugs.root_cause: Strict TypeScript rejects direct casts from Zustand/API module types to Jest mock types because the shapes do not overlap enough.
bugs.fix: Changed the test-only casts to go through unknown before narrowing to jest.Mock-typed objects.

bugs.error_message: apps/mobile npm audit reported high/moderate transitive vulnerabilities through Expo and React Native CLI packages after dependency install
bugs.root_cause: Expo SDK 51 and React Native CLI transitive dependencies pulled vulnerable versions of @xmldom/xmldom, fast-xml-parser, postcss, send, tar, and a dev-only @tootallnate/once chain.
bugs.fix: Added npm overrides for the vulnerable transitive packages and reran npm install; npm audit now reports 0 vulnerabilities.

bugs.error_message: Root/web npm audit reported Next.js, @supabase/ssr cookie, and eslint-config-next transitive advisories
bugs.root_cause: The current web stack is pinned to Next 14.2.35, @supabase/ssr 0.3.0, and eslint-config-next 14.2.35. npm's remaining fixes require breaking upgrades to Next/eslint-config-next 16.x and @supabase/ssr 0.10.x.
bugs.fix: Ran non-force npm audit fix to update compatible transitive packages and reran web lint/build/type-check successfully. Remaining advisories are not force-fixed because they require a planned Next/Supabase major compatibility pass.

bugs.error_message: Significant refactor of
bugs.root_cause: 2 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 37→49 lines (2 removed)

bugs.error_message: Incorrect value in code
bugs.root_cause: Had "Dockerfile"
bugs.fix: Changed to "apps/api/Dockerfile"

bugs.error_message: Incorrect value in code
bugs.root_cause: Had "apps/api/Dockerfile"
bugs.fix: Changed to "Dockerfile"

bugs.error_message: API service deployments all FAILING: Railway using apps/web/Dockerfile for api service because rootDirectory is null and root railway.toml points to web Dockerfile
bugs.root_cause: Railway reads root railway.toml for API service (rootDirectory: null). Root railway.toml is configured for web service. Fix: deploy via railway up apps/api --path-as-root --service api, and change dockerfilePath in apps/api/railway.toml from apps/api/Dockerfile to Dockerfile.
bugs.fix: Changed dockerfilePath from apps/api/Dockerfile to Dockerfile in apps/api/railway.toml. Deployed via railway up apps/api --path-as-root --service api. Permanent fix: set Root Directory = apps/api for api service in Railway Dashboard.

bugs.error_message: Mobile Jest warning cleanup initially broke rendering with React.createElement type undefined in TouchableOpacityMock
bugs.root_cause: The Jest setup mock imported an internal React Native View module path that resolves to undefined under jest-expo.
bugs.fix: Changed the TouchableOpacity Jest mock to render a plain host test View element, preserving press props without scheduling React Native TouchableOpacity animation timers.

bugs.error_message: SECURITY_REVIEW 2026-05-14: POST /v1/staff/invite allowed non-GM users and trusted body hotel_id
bugs.root_cause: Invite route used get_current_user_no_hotel and selected current_user.hotel_id or body.hotel_id, allowing authenticated users to invite arbitrary roles into arbitrary hotels.
bugs.fix: GM-gated /staff/invite with require_role("gm"), ignored body hotel_id for normal invites, added /staff/onboarding-invite that verifies active GM ownership before accepting body hotel_id, and updated onboarding to use it.

bugs.error_message: SECURITY_REVIEW 2026-05-14: Opera OAuth callback trusted raw hotel_id state
bugs.root_cause: OAuth connect used hotel_id directly as state, and unauthenticated callback upserted Opera credentials for that raw state value.
bugs.fix: Added one-time opera_oauth_states nonce table, generated random state in connect, validated unused non-expired nonce in callback, and used bound tenant_id for credential storage.

bugs.error_message: SECURITY_REVIEW 2026-05-14: Work-order update/delete endpoints were too permissive
bugs.root_cause: PATCH and DELETE accepted any authenticated hotel user, with only a cancellation special-case.
bugs.fix: Gated PATCH to engineer/chief_engineer/gm, DELETE to chief_engineer/gm, and restricted engineers to assigned or open unassigned work orders; completion now requires engineer assignment.

bugs.error_message: SECURITY_REVIEW 2026-05-14: Production web dependency audit reported Next and Supabase SSR advisories
bugs.root_cause: Web used Next 14.2.35 and @supabase/ssr 0.3.x, both in vulnerable production dependency ranges.
bugs.fix: Upgraded @supabase/ssr to 0.10.3 and Next to 16.3.0-canary.19, migrated middleware to proxy, updated Next config/server Supabase cookies compatibility, and verified production audit/build/type-check.

bugs.error_message: SECURITY_REVIEW 2026-05-14: Cross-tenant UUID references were accepted before inserts/upserts
bugs.root_cause: Service-role Supabase queries inserted foreign IDs without tenant-scoped existence checks.
bugs.fix: Added tenant-scoped prechecks for rooms, departments, staff, assets, shifts, and housekeepers before task/work-order/assignment/shift inserts and relevant updates.

bugs.error_message: SECURITY_REVIEW 2026-05-14: SOP uploads manually scraped Supabase localStorage auth token
bugs.root_cause: Multipart upload paths bypassed apiClient and read a specific localStorage key, which breaks with SSR cookie auth and cold-load sessions.
bugs.fix: Made apiClient FormData-aware without forcing Content-Type and routed SOP upload paths through apiClient session token lookup.

bugs.error_message: SECURITY_REVIEW 2026-05-14: SOP upload lacked size cap and filename sanitization
bugs.root_cause: Upload route accepted raw filenames in storage paths and read full file content into memory before any size check.
bugs.fix: Rejected path/control characters, sanitized PDF filenames, enforced a 10 MB limit while reading in chunks, and added regression tests.

bugs.error_message: SECURITY_REVIEW 2026-05-14: Some endpoints leaked internal error details
bugs.root_cause: Invalid JWT, health DB failure, and generic AI copilot errors returned raw exception text to clients.
bugs.fix: Logged detailed errors server-side and returned generic client-safe messages for invalid tokens, health DB status, and AI copilot failures.

bugs.error_message: Supabase CLI db query verification initially failed with unknown flag: --sql; OpenWolf log append also hit PowerShell quoting around select example
bugs.root_cause: Current Supabase CLI expects ad hoc SQL as a positional argument, not --sql; PowerShell parsed the inline documentation string with embedded select text incorrectly.
bugs.fix: Reran verification as `npx supabase db query "select ..." --linked` and appended the cerebrum note with single-quoted PowerShell string syntax.

bugs.error_message: Parallel verification command timed out while running supabase migration list alongside other checks
bugs.root_cause: The parallel tool call waited on a Supabase migration-list process that exceeded the tool timeout even though the table verification query completed successfully.
bugs.fix: Relied on the earlier successful migration repair/list output and direct table existence verification for final status.

bugs.error_message: Next 16 upgrade made the web lint script/config incompatible and surfaced render-purity errors
bugs.root_cause: Next 16 no longer supports the old next lint command shape, ESLint 9 requires flat config, and the new React compiler lint rules flagged Date.now calls during render.
bugs.fix: Changed the lint script to eslint ., added a Next 16 ESLint flat config that preserves existing project tolerance for legacy any/effect-state patterns, and moved Date.now usages into one-time useState initializers. npm run lint now exits 0.

bugs.error_message: Production smoke tooling initially failed: Railway service 'web' and old service IDs were not found; PowerShell Invoke-WebRequest did not support -SkipHttpErrorCheck.
bugs.file: developer tooling / Railway MCP / local shell commands
bugs.root_cause: The linked Railway project exposes the web service as '@patelrep/web' rather than 'web', the stored service IDs were stale for CLI lookup in this context, and this Windows PowerShell version lacks the -SkipHttpErrorCheck parameter.
bugs.fix: Used Railway service names 'api' and '@patelrep/web' for deployment checks and switched HTTP probes to curl.exe status/redirect/timing output.

bugs.error_message: Plain pytest command failed: pytest is not recognized on PATH
bugs.root_cause: API test runner is installed in the project virtualenv, not globally on PATH
bugs.fix: Use apps/api/.venv/Scripts/python -m pytest tests/ for local verification; command passed 110 tests

bugs.error_message: Python API lint check failed: No module named ruff
bugs.root_cause: ruff is not installed in the API virtualenv
bugs.fix: Not fixed in this verification pass; install/add ruff before requiring Python lint as a gate

bugs.error_message: Python API type check failed: No module named pyright
bugs.root_cause: pyright is not installed in the API virtualenv
bugs.fix: Not fixed in this verification pass; install/add pyright before requiring Python type checking as a gate

bugs.error_message: Tracked secret scan failed: credential-like material found in tracked files
bugs.root_cause: A private key block, a JWT-like public Supabase env value, and a hardcoded test password are present in tracked files
bugs.fix: Not fixed in this verification pass; remove tracked private key/local plugin data, review env production tracking, and move test password to env/fixture config

bugs.error_message: Fixed tracked secret scan failure from credential-like material in source-controlled files
bugs.file: .obsidian/plugins/obsidian-local-rest-api/data.json; apps/web/.env.production; e2e/auth.setup.ts; e2e/mobile-usability.spec.ts; e2e/helpers/rbac-users.ts; apps/api/tests/load/load_test.py; .gitignore; apps/web/.gitignore
bugs.root_cause: Local plugin state and concrete production env values were tracked, and test/load scripts contained hardcoded password fallbacks.
bugs.fix: Deleted tracked local plugin data and web .env.production, added ignore rules, and changed Playwright/RBAC/load-test authentication to require TEST_PASSWORD or RBAC_TEST_PASSWORD from the environment.

bugs.error_message: Ruff now runs locally and reports F401 unused import in tests/smoke/test_health.py
bugs.root_cause: The Python lint gate was previously unavailable; once installed it surfaced an unused import main.
bugs.fix: Not fixed in this answer; remove the unused import or run ruff --fix.

bugs.error_message: Pyright now runs locally and reports 139 existing type errors
bugs.root_cause: The Python type gate was previously unavailable; once installed it surfaced a broad existing baseline around Supabase optional responses, SDK stubs, and tests.
bugs.fix: Not fixed in this answer; triage pyright baseline separately before treating it as a blocking gate.

bugs.error_message: Fixed local API static gates after installing ruff and pyright
bugs.root_cause: Ruff found one unused import, and Pyright needed an API-local baseline config to exclude nested virtualenvs and suppress existing Supabase/SDK typing-noise buckets.
bugs.fix: Removed the unused test import and added pyrightconfig.json with include/exclude and baseline diagnostic settings. Ruff, Pyright, and API tests now pass.

bugs.error_message: Load-test simulation exited before traffic because TEST_PASSWORD is not set
bugs.root_cause: The staff-distribution simulation authenticates through Supabase and now requires TEST_PASSWORD from the environment after secret-remediation work removed hardcoded fallback credentials.
bugs.fix: Not fixed in this turn; export TEST_PASSWORD (and TEST_EMAIL if not using the default GM test account) before running the production load simulation.

bugs.error_message: PowerShell path search failed because route-group path apps/web/app/(dashboard)/settings/page.tsx was unquoted
bugs.root_cause: PowerShell treats parentheses in unquoted paths as expression syntax, matching an existing project gotcha.
bugs.fix: Use quoted paths for route-group directories in subsequent shell commands.

bugs.error_message: Load simulation using raw Playwright access token produced 100% 4xx responses
bugs.root_cause: The saved Playwright access token can be stale for direct API load tests even though the browser session can refresh it.
bugs.fix: Added Playwright storage-state session extraction and Supabase refresh-token flow before simulation requests. Rerun produced 297 requests with 0% 5xx and expected GM-token /my-rooms 4xx only.

bugs.error_message: Local screenshot script failed: browserContext.addCookies Cookie should have either url or path
bugs.root_cause: The script passed both url and path when adapting the saved production auth cookie to localhost.
bugs.fix: Rerun with only url on the cookie object.

bugs.error_message: Local visual verification timed out waiting for networkidle on /staff
bugs.root_cause: The staff page keeps background requests active long enough that networkidle is too strict for visual capture.
bugs.fix: Use domcontentloaded plus a short settle wait for visual verification.

bugs.error_message: API tests failed: test_integrations_security.py still expected removed Opera auth-code callback flow
bugs.root_cause: The OPERA integration was changed to credential-based OHIP connect, but the smoke tests still called opera_connect without a body and expected an opera_callback endpoint that no longer exists.
bugs.fix: Replaced stale callback tests with credential-based connect coverage and controlled OHIP credential-error assertions.

bugs.error_message: Ruff failed with F401 unused settings import in integrations.py and E402 import-order issue in webhooks.py
bugs.root_cause: The integrations router no longer used settings after the OPERA connect rewrite, and webhooks.py assigned logger before importing services.opera.webhooks.
bugs.fix: Removed the unused settings import and moved logger initialization below all imports.

bugs.error_message: PowerShell OPERA env-presence probe failed because $env:$_ interpolation is invalid
bugs.root_cause: PowerShell parsed $env:$_ as an invalid drive-qualified variable reference inside a string.
bugs.fix: Reran the check with Get-Item -Path "Env:\$_" and only printed boolean presence values.

bugs.error_message: OPERA integration would store integration_password, access_token, and refresh_token as plaintext in opera_credentials
bugs.root_cause: The credential-based OPERA connect flow wrote raw OHIP integration credentials and tokens directly into text columns, despite schema comments saying tokens should be encrypted.
bugs.fix: Added app-layer Fernet envelope encryption with enc:v1 prefix, production OPERA_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY requirement, backward-compatible decrypt of old plaintext rows, and tests that assert stored values are not raw secrets.

bugs.error_message: OPERA reservation sync could silently return zero synced reservations on OHIP auth/timeout failures
bugs.root_cause: ohip_request returned an empty dict for missing token, timeouts, and 401/404 responses, so sync_reservations could proceed as if Oracle returned no reservations and update last_sync_at.
bugs.fix: Introduced OperaRequestError with safe public messages, logged non-secret request metadata, and made sync_reservations return actionable errors without updating last_sync_at on OHIP request failure.

bugs.error_message: PowerShell rg command failed when using apps/api/tests/smoke/test_*opera* path pattern
bugs.root_cause: PowerShell passed the wildcard path in a way that rg treated as an invalid Windows filename.
bugs.fix: Relied on direct file-targeted checks and reran API Ruff/Pyright/pytest verification successfully.

bugs.error_message: New API rate-limit tests failed because middleware.rate_limit did not exist and legacy SlowAPI wiring was unused
bugs.root_cause: SlowAPI was installed and partially registered, but no effective global/default limiter or project middleware existed for forwarded-IP/user/IP route-tier limits.
bugs.fix: Added RateLimitMiddleware with route tiers, forwarded-client IP handling, 429 contract headers, config knobs, and tests; removed stale SlowAPI registration from main.py.

bugs.error_message: Focused API rate-limit test failed: anonymous AI route second request returned 200 instead of 429
bugs.root_cause: After separating verified-user shared-IP buckets, anonymous requests used the broad anonymous IP rule without also applying the stricter route tier.
bugs.fix: Use the stricter of anonymous and route rules for unauthenticated IP buckets, while verified users keep a high shared-IP bucket plus a per-user route bucket.

bugs.error_message: PowerShell anatomy slice command failed: Select-Object could not convert Index value "930..1000" to Int32
bugs.root_cause: PowerShell range expressions must be evaluated before passing to -Index; the unparenthesized range was parsed as a string.
bugs.fix: Reran with Select-Object -Index (930..1005).

bugs.error_message: After restoring pre-existing OPERA dirty files, API tests failed because test_integrations_security.py expected removed OAuth callback flow
bugs.root_cause: The restored smoke test was stale relative to the current credential-based OPERA connect endpoint, and restored routers had two Ruff issues.
bugs.fix: Updated the OPERA smoke test to cover credential-based connect and controlled errors, removed unused settings import, and moved logger initialization below imports.

bugs.error_message: Current checkout stored OPERA integration_password, access_token, and refresh_token as plaintext before using real credentials
bugs.root_cause: The prior app-layer encryption helper was missing from the working tree and OPERA connect/refresh paths wrote raw OHIP credentials directly into opera_credentials text columns.
bugs.fix: Restored Fernet enc:v1 envelope encryption, production OPERA_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY guard, decrypt-on-read compatibility, encrypted token refresh writes, disconnect password clearing, explicit cryptography dependency, and smoke assertions that stored secrets are encrypted.

bugs.error_message: Railway deploy failed: Cannot find module '/app/server.js' (web) + api service using wrong Dockerfile (apps/web/Dockerfile instead of apps/api/Dockerfile)
bugs.root_cause: 1) next upgraded from ^14.2.35 to ^16.3.0-canary.19 in May 16 commit — canary changes standalone output structure so server.js not found at /app/server.js. 2) api service rootDirectory was null so git-triggered deploys read root railway.toml (apps/web/Dockerfile) instead of apps/api/railway.toml
bugs.fix: 1) Reverted next and eslint-config-next to ^14.2.35 in apps/web/package.json. 2) Set api service rootDirectory to 'apps/api' via Railway GraphQL API (serviceInstanceUpdate mutation) so it reads apps/api/railway.toml on git-triggered deploys
